sourceMovie.title: Ip Man
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Ip Man for the same reason it broke through in 2008: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a action rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Wilson Yip keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Dragon: The Bruce Lee Story
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ip Man clicked for you, Dragon: The Bruce Lee Story (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ip Man?
faq.answer: Try Dragon: The Bruce Lee Story, Legend of the Fist: The Return of Chen Zhen, The Last Samurai—each shares DNA with Ip Man while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ip Man?
faq.answer: Ip Man (2008) is best described as Drama, Action, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ip Man part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ip Man Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
